Metadata.title: NEURON — Decision Intelligence for Product Teams
Metadata.description: NEURON is an AI-powered Decision System of Record that ingests signals from Slack, Jira, Notion, user interviews, and more — then uses multi-agent AI to synthesize insights and produce decision-ready artifacts.
Metadata.ogDescription: Stop burying decisions in noise. NEURON synthesizes product signals into decision-ready artifacts with multi-agent AI.
Metadata.productDescription: Explore NEURON's AI-powered features: signal ingestion, multi-agent synthesis, decision artifacts, and full traceability.
Metadata.whyNeuronDescription: Learn why product teams choose NEURON to solve the decision synthesis bottleneck and ship with clarity.
Metadata.integrationsDescription: NEURON integrates with Slack, Jira, Notion, Linear, GitHub, and more. It doesn't replace your tools — it connects them.
Metadata.pricingDescription: Simple, transparent pricing for teams of every size. Start free, upgrade as you grow.
Metadata.blogDescription: Insights on decision intelligence, product management, and multi-agent AI from the NEURON team.
Metadata.getDemoDescription: See how NEURON transforms product signals into decision-ready artifacts. Request a personalized demo for your team.

Hero.h2: NEURON is an AI decision system that captures decisions and their reasoning — aligning teams from strategy to execution.
Hero.trustLine: Trusted by product teams at fast-moving B2B SaaS companies

TrustBar.heading: Connects To The Tools You Already Use

Problem.heading: Modern Teams Don't Fail From Lack Of Data.
Problem.subheading: They fail because decisions get buried in noise.
Problem.pain1Description: PMs spend 30–50% of their time summarizing and re-aligning stakeholders instead of making decisions.
Problem.pain2Description: Decisions are re-litigated because context is lost in Slack threads and doc comments that no one can find.
Problem.pain3Description: Executives can't see WHY decisions were made — only the output. Strategy reviews become guesswork.

Features.heading: Your <highlight>Unfair Advantage</highlight> In Product Decisions
Features.tab1Title: Stop losing context in scattered tools
Features.tab1Description: Feedback lives everywhere—Slack threads, Jira comments, Notion pages, support tickets. Teams miss signals, duplicate work, and argue from incomplete info. NEURON captures it all automatically, so the full picture is always available.
Features.tab1Bullet3: Sync Notion docs, interviews, support, NPS, analytics
Features.tab2Title: Stop rehashing the same debate every week
Features.tab2Description: Most teams don't lack data—they lack clarity. Without structure, "insights" become opinions, loud voices win, and assumptions go unchallenged. NEURON uses multiple AI agents to pressure-test conclusions and surface the real tradeoffs—backed by evidence.
Features.tab2Bullet1: Agents validate and challenge each other
Features.tab2Bullet3: Bias + assumption flags, with sources linked
Features.tab3Title: Stop spending PM time turning talk into docs
Features.tab3Description: After alignment meetings, PMs still have to write the PRD, rewrite the rationale, and translate decisions into tickets—often multiple times. NEURON turns signals into ship-ready outputs your team can review, edit, and use immediately.
Features.tab3Bullet3: Prioritized ticket drafts; export to Jira / Linear / Notion
Features.tab4Title: Stop losing the "why" six months later
Features.tab4Description: When context fades, teams re-litigate decisions, new hires can't understand past calls, and leadership reviews become guesswork. NEURON stores decision history with full traceability—so anyone can see what was decided and why.
Features.tab4Bullet3: Searchable decision history for onboarding and audits

HowItWorks.heading: How It Works
HowItWorks.subheading: From signal to ship in three simple steps.
HowItWorks.step1Description: Link your Slack, Jira, Notion workspace in minutes. NEURON starts ingesting signals immediately.
HowItWorks.step2Description: Multi-agent AI analyzes signals and drafts decision artifacts — with adversarial validation built in.
HowItWorks.step3Description: Export PRDs and tickets back to Jira/Linear/Notion with full traceability. Ship with confidence.

StakeholderValue.heading: Built For Every Decision-Maker On Your Team
StakeholderValue.description1: See the full evidence trail behind every product decision. Run strategy reviews backed by data, not opinions. Ensure consistency across squads.
StakeholderValue.description2: Stop relying on summaries of summaries. Get direct visibility into why teams are building what they're building — with the receipts to back it up.
StakeholderValue.description3: Stop writing PRDs from scratch. Stop re-explaining decisions. NEURON drafts artifacts and maintains context so you can focus on what matters — shipping.

StatsBar.stat2Label: Hours saved on alignment per week

Testimonials.quote1: NEURON gave us a source of truth for why we built what we built. Our board reviews have never been cleaner.

FinalCTA.heading: Stop Losing Decisions To Noise.

WhyNeuronPage.heroDescription: Product teams drown in signals from Slack, Jira, user interviews, and analytics. NEURON transforms that noise into clear, traceable decisions — so you ship the right thing, not just the next thing.

IntegrationDetails.heading: All Integrations
IntegrationDetails.slackDescription: Captures feedback, discussions, and decisions from channels and threads in real-time.
IntegrationDetails.jiraDescription: Syncs tickets, comments, sprint data, and status changes for full development context.
IntegrationDetails.notionDescription: Ingests documents, databases, meeting notes, and product specs from your workspace.
IntegrationDetails.linearDescription: Pulls issues, project updates, and cycle data for engineering-aligned decision making.
IntegrationDetails.githubDescription: Monitors pull requests, issues, code reviews, and release notes for technical signals.

IntegrationDetails.salesforceDescription: Pulls deal insights, customer feedback, and pipeline signals to connect sales context with product decisions.
IntegrationDetails.servicenowDescription: Captures IT workflows, incident data, and change requests to bring operational context into product decisions.
IntegrationDetails.dropboxDescription: Syncs shared files, documents, and collaborative content to surface decision-relevant materials from your team's storage.
IntegrationDetails.hubspotDescription: Ingests customer feedback, deal signals, and support tickets for voice-of-customer insights.
IntegrationDetails.discordDescription: Captures community feedback, feature requests, and user discussions from your server.
IntegrationDetails.figmaDescription: Tracks design comments, version history, and collaboration signals from your design files.
IntegrationDetails.klaviyoDescription: Ingests email and SMS campaign performance, customer segments, and engagement signals for marketing-informed product decisions.
IntegrationDetails.abtastyDescription: Pulls A/B test results, feature flag data, and experimentation insights to back product decisions with real user behavior.
IntegrationDetails.hotjarDescription: Captures heatmaps, session recordings, and user feedback surveys to surface UX signals and behavioral patterns.
IntegrationDetails.usertestingDescription: Ingests user research sessions, video feedback, and usability test results to ground decisions in real user voices.
IntegrationDetails.statsigDescription: Connects feature flag rollouts, experiment results, and product metrics to link shipping decisions with measured impact.
IntegrationDetails.comingSoonDescription: More application connectors coming soon — and by enterprise request. Let us know what your team needs.

BlogPage.heroDescription: Perspectives on product management, multi-agent AI, and the future of how teams make decisions.

Blog.article1Title: How Multi-Agent AI Transforms Product Decisions
Blog.article1Excerpt: Single-model AI gives you answers. Multi-agent AI gives you answers that have survived scrutiny. Here's why adversarial validation changes everything.
Blog.article1Body:
Traditional AI systems use a single model to analyze data and produce recommendations. The problem? A single perspective — no matter how sophisticated — carries inherent blind spots. Multi-agent AI takes a fundamentally different approach. Multiple specialized agents each analyze the same signals from different angles: one focuses on user sentiment, another on technical feasibility, a third on business impact. These agents then challenge each other's conclusions through adversarial validation — the same way a well-run product team pressure-tests ideas before committing.

The result is recommendations that have been stress-tested before they reach you. When NEURON's synthesis engine surfaces a product insight, it comes with confidence scores, dissenting perspectives, and the evidence trail behind each conclusion. You're not just getting an answer — you're getting an answer that has survived scrutiny from multiple angles.

This matters because product decisions are rarely black and white. The best decisions account for tradeoffs that a single model might miss: the feature that tests well with users but creates technical debt, or the strategic pivot that looks risky in isolation but makes sense when you see the full competitive landscape. Multi-agent synthesis surfaces these nuances automatically, so your team spends less time debating and more time shipping.
Blog.article2Title: The Cost of Decision Debt in Product Teams
Blog.article2Excerpt: Every decision that isn't documented, traced, or validated creates compounding debt. We calculated what that costs the average product team.
Blog.article2Body:
We've all heard of technical debt — the shortcuts and workarounds that accumulate in code over time. But there's an equally damaging form of debt that product teams rarely talk about: decision debt. Decision debt accumulates every time a team makes a choice without documenting the reasoning, every time context is lost in a Slack thread that nobody can find three months later, and every time a new team member has to re-discover why something was built a certain way.

Our research across dozens of product teams found staggering numbers. The average PM spends 30-50% of their time on what we call "alignment theater" — re-explaining decisions, re-gathering context, and re-litigating choices that were already made. That's not just wasted time; it's compounding waste. Each undocumented decision creates a ripple effect: future decisions that depend on it also lack context, leading to more meetings, more re-litigation, and more misalignment.

The solution isn't more documentation processes — teams already have too many. The solution is making decision capture automatic. When every signal, discussion, and conclusion is traced and searchable, decision debt stops accumulating. New hires can onboard in days instead of weeks. Leadership reviews become evidence-based instead of opinion-based. And PMs can finally spend their time on what they were hired to do: making great product decisions.
Blog.article3Title: From Signals to Ship: A NEURON Workflow Guide
Blog.article3Excerpt: A step-by-step guide to setting up NEURON, connecting your tools, and producing your first decision artifact in under 30 minutes.
Blog.article3Body:
Getting started with NEURON takes less time than your average standup. Here's how to go from zero to your first decision artifact in under 30 minutes.

Step 1: Connect your tools. Head to the Integrations page and link your Slack workspace, Jira project, and Notion workspace. Each connection takes about 2 minutes and requires standard OAuth permissions. NEURON begins ingesting signals immediately — no historical data import needed, though you can enable it for richer context.

Step 2: Define your first decision scope. Tell NEURON what you're deciding on. This could be a feature prioritization, a strategic pivot, or a technical architecture choice. NEURON uses this scope to filter and weight the signals it ingests, ensuring the synthesis is focused and relevant.

Step 3: Review the synthesis. Within minutes, NEURON's multi-agent engine produces a decision brief that includes: a summary of relevant signals from all connected sources, synthesized insights with confidence scores, identified tradeoffs and risks, and a draft recommendation. You can edit, annotate, and share this brief directly — or export it as a PRD, Jira tickets, or a strategy memo.

Step 4: Ship with traceability. Every artifact NEURON produces maintains a full lineage back to the original signals. Six months from now, anyone on your team can trace exactly why a decision was made, what evidence supported it, and what alternatives were considered.
Blog.article4Title: Why Traceability Matters More Than You Think
Blog.article4Excerpt: When someone asks 'why did we build this?' six months from now, you'll want a better answer than 'I think it was in a Slack thread somewhere.'
Blog.article4Body:
Every product team has experienced this moment: someone — a board member, a new VP, a curious engineer — asks "why did we build this feature?" And the room goes quiet. The PM who made the call left six months ago. The Slack thread where it was discussed has been buried under thousands of messages. The Notion doc that might have the rationale is in someone's private workspace. The answer becomes a best guess.

This isn't just embarrassing — it's expensive. Without traceability, teams re-litigate decisions that were already made, often reaching different conclusions the second time around. New hires spend weeks absorbing tribal knowledge that should be searchable. And leadership loses confidence in the product team's ability to make strategic, evidence-based calls.

Traceability means every decision has a clear lineage: the signals that informed it, the discussions that shaped it, the evidence that supported it, and the alternatives that were considered. It's not about creating more documentation — it's about making the documentation that matters happen automatically. When decision history is searchable and traceable, onboarding accelerates, accountability improves, and your team builds institutional memory that compounds over time instead of evaporating with each departure.
